description:
Step 4 of T-012's 4-step CSP roadmap. Depends on T-031 (Step 2 inline handlers) and T-032 (Step 3 inline styles) being complete, OR the explicit decision in T-032 to accept `'unsafe-inline'` for style-src only.

**Changes:**
1. **In-repo:** edit `app/middleware/csp_nonce.py:_CSP_TEMPLATE` to drop `'unsafe-inline'` from `script-src` (and from `style-src` if T-032's decision allows). Rename header from `Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only` to `Content-Security-Policy` (enforcing).
2. **On VPS (`/opt/edge/nginx/conf.d/vigilant.conf:47`):** DELETE the static `add_header Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only ...` line entirely. The app's per-request header replaces it. Reload edge nginx (`docker exec edge-nginx-1 nginx -s reload`).

**Verification gate (don't skip):**
- Open browser devtools on every major page (dashboard, fitting tool, star map, killmails, admin, intel pages, structure timers, account settings).
- Confirm zero CSP errors in the console.
- Test every major action that uses htmx partials (button clicks, form submits, dropdown changes).
- Test once with a hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) to ensure no cached pages with old inline handlers slip through.

**Rollback plan:** if violations show up post-flip, immediately revert the app commit (`git revert <sha> && deploy.sh`). The CSP middleware change is cleanly reversible — the nonce annotations on templates stay (they're inert under Report-Only).

**Closes VVP-2026-007** after deploy + verification.</description>
title: CSP Step 4: drop unsafe-inline + flip Report-Only to enforcing (VVP-2026-007)
